steps.assistant_text: uso_find_slash (kernel, 0x54 / 21 insns): scan a NUL-terminated string for '/' and write the index to *arg1, return 1 if found else 0. Disassembled the target from expected/src/kernel/kernel_000.c.o (objdump -d 0x20c..0x260). The structure is a do/while where index v0 trails v1 by one statement: at loop entry v0==v1; the body loads arg0[v0], increments v1, computes &arg0[v1] (the continuation char) BEFORE the '/' test, and the bnel back-branch + the entry beqz both fill their delay slots with `or v0,v1,zero`. The old NM wrap (96B, ~58%) had v0 init split from the carry, so IDO put `move v0,zero` before the entry branch and filled the delay with the loop-invariant `li a2,0x2F` constant, and the loop body emitted v1++ AFTER the '/' branch (3 insns of divergence). Rewriting as `v1=0; v0=v1; if(arg0[v0]) do{...; v1++; if(arg0[v0]=='/'){...} v0=v1;}while(arg0[v1]);` brought the whole loop body and registers byte-exact, leaving exactly TWO delay-slot-filler diffs (entry beqz delay + loop-back bnel delay: target `or v0,v1` vs mine const/addr-recompute) — a pure scheduling tie. Ran decomp-permuter (IDO -O2, no PERM macros) from base score 285; it found score=0 by adding a duplicate `v0 = v1;` at the TOP of the loop body. That extra copy is the value-numbering nudge that makes IDO schedule `or v0,v1` into both delay slots. build/.o vs expected/.o func region 0x20c..0x260: 21 words / 84 bytes byte-identical. Full `make clean && make` ROM byte-identical to baserom (cmp tenshoe.z64 baserom.z64 clean; make verify: ROM OK).

metadata.verification: build/.o vs expected/.o func region 0x20c..0x260: 21 words (84 bytes) byte-identical; full `make clean && make` ROM byte-identical to baserom (cmp tenshoe.z64 baserom.z64 clean; make verify OK). Solved via decomp-permuter (score 285 -> 0).
